title: Lebanese Red Lentil Soup Adas

seo.title: Authentic Lebanese Red Lentil Soup (Adas) Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Discover how to make the best authentic Lebanese Red Lentil Soup (Adas) with this easy-to-follow recipe. Healthy, flavorful, and perfect for any meal!

seo.h1: Authentic Lebanese Red Lentil Soup (Adas) Recipe
h2Headings: What is Lebanese Red Lentil Soup Adas?
Step-by-Step Instructions for Lebanese Red Lentil Soup

seo.imageAlt: A bowl of authentic Lebanese Red Lentil Soup (Adas) garnished with fresh cilantro and a lemon wedge.
seo.imageCaption: Warm up with a comforting bowl of homemade Lebanese Red Lentil Soup (Adas), a traditional Middle Eastern delight.

description: Lebanese Red Lentil Soup, known as Adas, is a comforting and nutritious staple in Middle Eastern cuisine. This hearty vegetarian soup is celebrated for its vibrant flavor profile, combining earthy lentils with aromatic spices and a bright finish of fresh lemon. It is incredibly easy to prepare and perfect for a cozy meal any day of the week.

instructions.title: Prepare Ingredients
instructions.text: Rinse the red lentils thoroughly under cold water until the water runs clear. Drain well. Chop the onion, mince the garlic, and dice the carrots. This preparation ensures a smooth cooking process.

instructions.title: Sauté Aromatics
instructions.text: In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and sauté for 5-7 minutes until softened and translucent. Add the minced garlic and diced carrots, cooking for another 3-4 minutes until fragrant.

instructions.title: Add Lentils and Spices
instructions.text: Stir in the rinsed red lentils, cumin powder, turmeric powder, salt, and black pepper. Cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly to coat the lentils and toast the spices lightly, enhancing their flavor.

instructions.title: Simmer the Soup
instructions.text: Pour in 6 cups of vegetable broth or water. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils are completely tender and have broken down. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.

instructions.title: Adjust Consistency
instructions.text: If you prefer a smoother soup, use an immersion blender to partially or fully blend the soup directly in the pot. Alternatively, carefully transfer portions to a regular blender and blend until desired consistency is reached. Add more broth or water if the soup is too thick.

instructions.title: Finish and Serve
instructions.text: Stir in the fresh lemon juice. Taste and adjust seasonings as needed, adding more salt, pepper, or lemon juice to your preference. Ladle the hot soup into bowls and garnish generously with fresh chopped cilantro or parsley before serving.

tips: For an extra layer of flavor, prepare a 'ta'leeyeh' by frying a small amount of minced garlic and dried mint in olive oil until fragrant, then swirl it into the soup just before serving.
Do not overcook the lentils initially, as they will continue to soften. If you prefer a soup with more texture, blend only a portion of it.
Adas soup is best served hot with a drizzle of extra virgin olive oil and a side of warm pita bread or toasted croutons. It also freezes well for future enjoyment.
article: Lebanese Red Lentil Soup Adas is a beloved Middle Eastern Soups and Stews dish enjoyed across Middle Eastern. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
